records.ety: [L. basiliscus, Gr. basili`skos little king, kind of serpent, dim. of basiley`s king; -- so named from some prominences on the head resembling a crown.]

defs: A fabulous serpent, or dragon. The ancients alleged that its hissing would drive away all other serpents, and that its breath, and even its look, was fatal. See Cockatrice.
quotes: Make me not sighted like the basilisk.

defs: A lizard of the genus Basiliscus, belonging to the family Iguanidæ.
notes: ☞ This genus is remarkable for a membranous bag rising above the occiput, which can be filled with air at pleasure; also for an elevated crest along the back, that can be raised or depressed at will.

defs: A large piece of ordnance, so called from its supposed resemblance to the serpent of that name, or from its size.
